courses.title: Calculus for Science, Engineering, and Mathematics
Calculus for Science, Engineering, and Mathematics

notes: PREPARATION FOR THE MINOR CSE 12: Computer Systems and Assembly Language and Lab CSE 13S: Computer Systems and C Programming OR ECE 13: Computer Systems and C Programming CSE 16: Applied Discrete Mathematics CSE 20: Beginning Programming in Python CSE 30: Programming Abstractions: Python MATH 19A: Calculus for Science, Engineering, and Mathematics MATH 19B: Calculus for Science, Engineering, and Mathematics AM 20: Mathematical Methods for Engineers II OR MATH 24: Ordinary Differential Equations ECE 101: Introduction to Electronic Circuits AND ECE 101L: Introduction to Electronic Circuits Laboratory Plus one of the following lecture/lab combinations: PHYS 5A: Introduction to Physics I AND PHYS 5L: Introduction to Physics I Laboratory OR PHYS 6A: Introductory Physics I AND PHYS 6L: Introductory Physics I Laboratory Plus one of the following lecture/lab combinations: PHYS 5C: Introduction to Physics III AND PHYS 5N: Introduction to Physics III Laboratory OR PHYS 6C: Introductory Physics III AND PHYS 6N: Introductory Physics III Laboratory Please visit the Baskin Engineering Undergraduate Experience website to learn more about this minor.

UC Santa Cruz students may choose to complete one or more minors, provided they can finish all required coursework within their maximum enrollment terms and allowable credits. While a minor will appear on a student's official transcript, it will not be listed on their diploma. Pursuing a minor is a personal decision that should align with a student's educational and career goals. Students do not apply for minors when submitting their application to UC Santa Cruz. After enrolling, transfer students interested in pursuing a minor should contact the department offering the minor for guidance.
